feed.title: [BIP] P2WOTS: 64 Slot Winternitz UTXO's (witness version three)

entry.title: [BIP] P2WOTS: 64 Slot Winternitz UTXO's (witness version three)
entry.summary:
The recent proposal discussed within the Bitcoin development community has sparked significant criticism due to its shortcomings compared to BIP360. The primary concern is the lack of flexibility offered for a gradual migration process, which is essential for widespread adoption prior to Q-day. A critical omission in the proposal is the absence of usable EC spending paths, which are vital for encouraging early migration to the proposed PQ address format.
Another major flaw highlighted is the elimination of scripting capabilities post-Q-day. This change would result in the loss of several key functionalities within the Bitcoin network such as lightning networks, ARK, DLCs, timelocks, and covenants. The decision to limit the number of signatures to 64 without clear justification also raises questions about the adequacy and excessiveness of this threshold. Furthermore, the use of 256-bit hash functions is considered excessive, leading to unnecessarily large signature sizes that could be optimized by utilizing modern hash-based cryptography like SPHINCS, which employs tweaked hash functions for better efficiency.
Additional technical issues identified include numerous off-by-one errors in security-critical array indexing operations, inconsistencies in the hash functions used in signing and verification algorithms which may compromise the scheme's correctness and soundness, and a lack of clarity and definition regarding many variables within the proposal. Moreover, the representation of the 6-bit key index with a 32-byte witness element is questioned for its practicality.
The critique suggests that anyone looking to develop or endorse this BIP should first deepen their understanding of hash-based cryptography through relevant literature. This foundational knowledge is crucial to ensure any new proposals are not only theoretically sound but also practically viable for implementation within the Bitcoin ecosystem.
